bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Modest potential risk to estimates from tariffs.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We remain positive on software, especially given recent pullback, while acknowledging modest potential risk to estimates from tariffs.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Potential risk to current guidance (CY25) and possibility of an 'air-pocket' as the year unfolds.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We explore two key themes: 1) Potential risk to current guidance (CY25) and 2) Best positioned software companies. At best, Management teams will hold guidance unchanged near-term, although it’s possible we might enter an air-pocket as the year unfolds as we previously highlighted here.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Worst-case scenarios of -5% to -20% Net New Revenue (NNR) cuts, implying a 100-300 bps reduction in CY25 average revenue growth.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We’ve conducted a sensitivity analysis around net new revenue (NNR), assuming worst-case scenarios of -5% to -20% NNR cuts, though we do not subscribe to this outcome yet. This implies CY25 average revenue growth (GSe +13%) could see a 100–300 bps reduction (albeit -4% drawdown in USD since C1Q close vs. major currencies could be a partial mitigant).

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Recent software multiple compression.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We’re also adjusting our PTs to reﬂect recent software multiple compression but continue to highlight the disconnect between current valuations and fundamentals (GSe 46% discount to Intrinsic Value).

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Modest risk potential that Management teams may opt to provide more conservative guidance on C2Q and CY25 due to tariff uncertainty.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: we acknowledge the modest risk potential that Management teams may opt to provide more conservative guidance on C2Q (vs. Street and implied guidance) and CY25.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Management teams are likely to call out deal cycle elongation, reduced contract sizes, and/or broader freezes in software procurement.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: For subscription and consumption models alike, Management teams are likely to call out some level of deal cycle elongation, reduced contract sizes and/or broader freezes in software procurement entering the June quarter (April/May); at a minimum, they’re likely to acknowledge the risk of such a dynamic impacting upcoming quarters.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Downside scenario contemplates a slowdown in key drivers of the software growth algorithm (Net expansion, New logos, Customer retention) relative to Management guidance.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our downside scenario contemplates a slowdown in key drivers of the software growth algorithm relative to Management guidance. To determine the impact, we evaluated the implied Net New Revenue for CY25 based on Management guidance and reduced that assumption anywhere from -5% to -20%, which we see as an appropriate starting point given broad uncertainty as to what shape tariff policy ultimate takes in the comings days, weeks and months. We believe this approach also effectively captures the potential degradation to all or some critical components of the software growth algorithm: 1) Net expansion, 2) New logos and 3) Customer retention.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Average revenue growth for coverage in CY25 could come down -70 bps to -280 bps with NNR cuts.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our net new revenue sensitivity analysis implies that average revenue growth for our coverage in CY25 (GSe: 13%) could come down -70 bps in a more conservative scenario of a -5% cut to NNR and upwards of -280 bps in a downside scenario where NNR is cut by -20% (Exhibit 2).

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Potential margin degradation if software companies are unable to dynamically adapt to a slower demand environment (e.g., if NNR cuts are not met with operating leverage).
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We also provide an illustrative downside scenario analysis where cuts to net new revenue are not met with a commensurate step-up in operating leverage. Assuming a worst-case scenario where CY25 NNR is cut by -10% vs. initial guidance and fully ﬂows through to the FCF line, our coverage would see an average ~100 bps reduction in CY25 FCFM’s and a ~200 bps decline in their ‘Rule of’ proﬁles (Exhibit 3). This downside case assumes no variable cost ﬂexibility and illustrates the potential margin degradation if software companies are unable to dynamically adapt to a slower demand environment.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Potential for modest cuts to net new revenue if trends softened post-tariff announcement.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our analysis of net new revenue trends pre- and post-guidance points suggests potential for modest cuts if trends softened post-tariff announcement.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Management teams adjusted to rising interest rates and macroeconomic uncertainty, leading to tightening IT budgets and prioritization of cost optimization over new digital initiatives.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: However, in both CY23 and CY24, NNR estimates came down -7% to -10% when company guidance was issued as Management teams adjusted to the new reality of rising interest rates and macroeconomic uncertainty, which led to tightening IT budgets and the relative prioritization of cost optimization over new digital initiatives.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: The software sector has been contending with a sluggish macro environment, leading to growth deceleration from +31% in CY22 to +15% in CY24.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The earliest representation of this dynamic began in C2H22, when net new business growth started to contract sharply for broader software (Exhibit 8). The sector has been contending with a sluggish macro ever since, representative in growth for our coverage decelerating from +31% in CY22 to +15% in CY24 (Exhibit 5).

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: The far-reaching implications of the new Administration’s tariff policy were an unforeseen variable.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: While there were reasons to be optimistic that CY25 would mark a year of growth stability – stabilizing NER, improving new logo acquisition and an emerging Gen-AI product cycle (Exhibit 6, Exhibit 7) – the far-reaching implications of the new Administration’s tariff policy was an unforeseen variable.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Net new revenue estimates for coverage have come down -1.5% since the announcement of tariffs.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We note that since the announcement of tariffs, net new revenue estimates for our coverage have come down -1 .5% (now -7 .1% vs. pre-guidance); this, however, is still well within the historical window Management teams typically guide to (-7-10%).

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Modest risk potential that Management teams incrementally de-risk expectations for 2Q and CY25 due to short-term demand fallout from tariff uncertainty.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As such, we acknowledge the modest risk potential that Management teams incrementally de-risk expectations for 2Q and CY25 during the upcoming March/April quarter reporting season to better factor for any short-term demand fallout from tariff uncertainty.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Tariff uncertainty may cause Management teams to revisit assumptions around Net Expansion Rate (NER) and new logo acquisition if there is demand degradation.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: However, we don’t rule out the possibility that the impact of tariffs may cause Management teams to revisit their assumptions around NER and new logo acquisition if there were to be any demand degradation in the month of April.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: The knock-on effect of tariffs could cause many enterprises to temporarily pause hiring plans, delay ongoing purchasing decisions, and/or generally pull back on more transformational projects.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: On net, we anticipate that the knock-on effect of tariffs could cause many enterprises to temporarily pause hiring plans (impacting software seat growth), delay ongoing purchasing decisions, and/or generally pull back on more transformational projects until tariff policies crystallize.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: In periods of uncertainty, software companies tend to observe varying degrees of degradation to cross-sell and upsell, new logo acquisition, and retention rates.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In periods of uncertainty, software companies tend to observe varying degrees of degradation to cross-sell and upsell, new logo acquisition and retention rates.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: 2025 may not mark a meaningful inflection point for Gen-AI given potential budgetary constraints against a more volatile macro backdrop.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Albeit, this year may not mark a meaningful inﬂection point for Gen-Al given potential budgetary constraints against a more volatile macro backdrop, but we do expect many software companies to foster engagement and adoption of products that can drive real productivity against across the enterprise.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: The market might be slow to participate in the Gen-AI theme until early signals of a 'killer application' emerge, as AI-native application companies have yet to reach public markets.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: AI-native application companies have yet to reach the public markets. While there are early signs of AI revenue contribution from some large public software vendors, the absence of application-layer Gen-Al-native private companies entering the public markets has left investors largely conﬁned to incumbents. As such, we note that the market might be slow to participate in the Gen-AI theme until early signals of an ‘killer application’ with meaningful utilization and monetization emerges, which we then believe can become a catalyst for the sector.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Investors hesitate to underwrite AI as a material revenue driver in the near-term due to macro and budgetary uncertainty, as most enterprises are still in experimentation/early roll-out phases.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: With most enterprises are still in the experimentation and early roll-out phases, we understand investors’ hesitation around underwriting AI as a material revenue driver in the near-term, particularly amidst broader macro and budgetary uncertainty.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Macroeconomic headwinds, tariff concerns, public sector austerity, and broader market volatility have weighed on investor sentiment and raised concerns around weakening enterprise software demand, leading to coverage underperforming NASDAQ.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our coverage is down -15% YTD, underperforming the NASDAQ by ~2 pp., as a combination of macroeconomic headwinds- tariff concerns, public sector austerity, and broader market volatility- has weighed on investor sentiment and raised concerns around weakening enterprise software demand.
